---
title: "SLAM Metabolic Class Figures"
author: "William Mueller"
data: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%Y %B %d')`"
output:
  html_document:
    df_print: paged
params:
  model_dir: "20220523_121216_test"
---

```{r setup, warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, echo=FALSE}
library(helphlme)
library(tidyverse)
library(kableExtra)
library(survival)
library(survminer)
library(ggplotify)
library(sjPlot)
library(lme4)
library(cowplot)
library(RColorBrewer)
# setting global knitr options for r markdown output
knitr::opts_chunk$set(autodep = TRUE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(warning = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(message = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
knitr::opts_chunk$set(cache = FALSE)



mega_pal <- rep(palette(), 40)

```

## Analysis

### Load Models

```{r load models}
path <- normalizePath(file.path("..", "04_model_select", "output", params$model_dir))
df_list_path <- normalizePath(file.path(path, "df_list.RDATA"))
final_models_path <- normalizePath(file.path(path, "final_models.RDATA"))

load(file = df_list_path)
load(file = final_models_path)

# load in main_cat_surv
load(file = "../04b_healthcard_cod/output/main_cat_surv.RDATA")
# load in health
load(file = "../04b_healthcard_cod/output/health.RDATA")
# load in dvr
load(file = "../04b_healthcard_cod/output/dvr.RDATA")
```

```{r create summary dataframe}
subject_sum <- main_cat_surv %>%
  select(idno,
         tag,
         sex,
         strain,
         dead_censor,
         fu_age_wk,
         le_wk,
         cod,
         lastdate,
         maxdate,
         tod) %>%
  mutate(idno = as.numeric(idno)) %>%
  left_join(dvr, by = "idno")

# check na's after merging
apply(apply(subject_sum, 2, is.na), 2, sum)

# obs goe from 1304 to 1310, so their are duplicates, lets find these duplicates
# and choose the correct one
id <- subject_sum$idno
subject_sum[duplicated(id) | duplicated(id, fromLast = TRUE),]

# if there a duplicates, we are going to take the observation with the cod.y
# that is not NA or Undetermined. If there is no NA or undetermined, we are 
# going to take the observation with the later dod
subject_sum <- subject_sum %>%
  group_by(idno) %>%
  # cod_effective is 0 if "undetermined" or is.na
  mutate(cod_effective = ifelse(cod.y == "Undetermined" | is.na(cod.y), 0, 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(cod_effective), desc(dod)) %>%
  distinct(idno, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  as.data.frame()

# now we have to add the health card information. lets convert it to wide format
# so that we can add it to the subject summary 
# create wave for health
health <- health %>%
  group_by(tag) %>%
  arrange(date_created) %>%
  mutate(wave =row_number()) %>%
  ungroup()
health_wide <- pivot_wider(health,
                       id_cols = c("idno", "tag", "dod"),
                       names_from = "wave",
                       names_prefix = "condition_",
                       values_from = "condition_clean") 

health_wide_heat <- pivot_wider(health,
                                id_cols = c("idno", "tag", "dod"),
                                names_from = "condition_clean",
                                values_from = "condition_clean")

health_wide_heat <- health_wide_heat 

col_of_interest <- 4:length(health_wide_heat)

health_wide_heat[, col_of_interest] <- lapply(health_wide_heat[, col_of_interest],
                                   function(col) {
                                     ifelse(col == "NULL", 0, 1)
                                   }) 

count <- as.data.frame(apply(health_wide_heat[, col_of_interest], 2, sum))

count

names(count) <- "n"
count$condition <- rownames(count)
rownames(count) <- NULL

# select interesting healthcard 
count <- arrange(count, -n)
write.csv(count, file = "output/conditions_and_counts.csv")
```

### Class Analysis

#### Create Census Dataframe
```{r census data frame}
# will number classes so that all classes of list given to function
# are labelled uniquely
make_census <- function(cf_row) {
  cf_row <- final_models[1,]
  model <- unlist(cf_row$final_model, recursive = FALSE)
  class_df <- model$pprob
  
  main <- df_list[[cf_row$data]]
  
  # add class_df to main
  census <- main %>%
    mutate(idno = as.numeric(idno)) %>%
    left_join(class_df, by = "idno") %>%
    distinct(idno, .keep_all = T) %>%
    select(class, idno, cohort, sex, strain, le_wk, starts_with("prob")) %>%
    filter(!is.na(class)) %>%
    mutate(idno = as.character(idno)) 
  
  # add survival information to census
  census <- census %>%
    left_join(select(main_cat_surv, cod, dead_censor, idno), by ="idno") %>%
    mutate(
      age_wk_death = le_wk,
      dead_nat = dead_censor
    )
  
  # return census
  census
}


final_models$census <- lapply(seq_len(nrow(final_models)), function(i) {
  cf_row <- final_models[i,]
  make_census(cf_row)
})


```


#### Create Table 1
```{r create table 1}

final_models$model_df <- lapply(final_models$data, function(data_name) {
  df_list[[data_name]]
})


final_models$census_surv_cod <- lapply(final_models$model_df, function(df) {
  
})

t1 <- function(df) {
  df_main <- df
  df_table <- df_main %>%
    group_by(class) %>%
    summarise(n = n()) %>%
    select(class, n, everything())

  df_count_cohort <- df_main %>%
    group_by(class) %>%
    count(cohort) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    spread(key = cohort, value = n, sep = "_")

  df_count_sex <- df_main %>%
    group_by(class) %>%
    count(sex) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    spread(key = sex, value = n, sep = "_")

  df_count_strain <- df_main %>%
    group_by(class) %>%
    count(strain) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    spread(key = strain, value = n, sep = "_")

  surv <- survfit(
    data = df_main,
    Surv(time = age_wk_death, event = dead_nat) ~ factor(class)
  )
  df_surv <- surv_median(surv) %>%
    rename(
      class = strata,
      median_surv = median
    ) %>%
    select(-lower, -upper)

  df_surv$class <- sort(unique(df_main$class))


  df_table <- df_table %>%
    left_join(df_count_cohort, by = "class") %>%
    left_join(df_count_sex, by = "class") %>%
    left_join(df_count_strain, by = "class") %>%
    left_join(df_surv, by = "class")

  df_table <- df_table %>%
    t() %>%
    data.frame() %>%
    round() %>%
    janitor::row_to_names(1)

  df_table[is.na(df_table)] <- 0


  names(df_table) <- paste0("class_", names(df_table))

  df_table$total <- rowSums(df_table[, ]) ## sum rows to create total column
  ## get set survival columns to NA
  df_table[grepl(rownames(df_table), pattern = "surv"), "total"] <- NA

  ## create new cols that are the frequency of the n columns
  new_cols <- do.call(cbind, lapply(1:(length(df_table) - 1), function(i) {
    new_col <- round(df_table[[i]] / df_table$total * 100, 1)
  })) %>%
    data.frame()

  names(new_cols) <- paste0(names(df_table[, -length(df_table)]), "_freq")

  df_table <- cbind(df_table, new_cols) ## bind freq columns to original table

  l <- length(df_table) ## number of columns of dataframe
  x <- (length(df_table) - 1) / 2 ## number of classes
  for (i in 1:x) {
    df_table[, i + l] <- paste0(
      as.character(df_table[, i]),
      " (",
      df_table[, i + x + 1],
      "%)"
    )
  }

  df_table <- as.data.frame(
    sapply(df_table,
      gsub,
      pattern = "(NA%)",
      replacement = "weeks"
    ),
    row.names = rownames(df_table)
  )
  return(df_table)
}

```
